tr.td: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 20 units (14 units NPH/6 units regular) subcutaneously before breakfast and 10 units (7 units NPH/3 units regular) before dinner
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
0.3 mg (1 mg/mL) injected intramuscularly as needed for severe allergic reaction
Hydrocortisone 10 mg/g and urea 100 mg/g cutaneous cream
Apply a thin layer (containing 10 mg/g hydrocortisone and 100 mg/g urea) to the affected area twice daily
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)

ul.li: Patient will participate in a diabetes self-management education program to improve understanding of prediabetes and reduce risk of progression to type 2 diabetes.
Patient will achieve a 5% reduction in body weight through dietary changes and increased physical activity.
Patient will maintain hemoglobin A1c below 5.8% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Patient will maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle changes to help control blood pressure, including dietary modifications, increased physical activity, and reduced sodium intake.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg.
Maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg.
Reduce body weight to decrease severity of obstructive sleep apnea symptoms
Maintain blood pressure within target range to reduce cardiovascular risk associated with obstructive sleep apnea
Achieve at least 7 hours of sleep per night to improve daytime functioning and reduce OSA symptoms
Use CPAP device for at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights to optimize OSA management

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Glucose [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip
pH of Urine by Test strip
Protein [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Protein [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

ul.li: Patient will participate in a diabetes self-management education program to improve understanding of prediabetes and reduce risk of progression to type 2 diabetes.
Patient will achieve a 5% reduction in body weight through dietary changes and increased physical activity.
Patient will maintain hemoglobin A1c below 5.8% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Patient will maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle changes to help control blood pressure, including dietary modifications, increased physical activity, and reduced sodium intake.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg.
Maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg.
Reduce body weight to decrease severity of obstructive sleep apnea symptoms
Maintain blood pressure within target range to reduce cardiovascular risk associated with obstructive sleep apnea
Achieve at least 7 hours of sleep per night to improve daytime functioning and reduce OSA symptoms
Use CPAP device for at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights to optimize OSA management
